content.claim: Finland's shift from a two-tier grammar/civic system to comprehensive schooling (1972–1977) was associated with rising academic performance by the 1980s–1990s and historically low between-school variance, but this occurred alongside simultaneous reforms in teacher training, curriculum design, and welfare-state expansion
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Causal attribution to comprehensive structure alone cannot be cleanly separated from concurrent reforms in teacher professionalization and curriculum
Performance gains took approximately two decades to appear in assessments
Finnish domestic criticism about the 'levelling effect' of comprehensive schooling was widespread until PISA 2001 validation, indicating initial uncertainty about the model's efficacy
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
